summary: applyMigrations is defined but NEVER called anywhere in the codebase
details: applyMigrations() in db.pg.ts is only re-exported from node.ts but never invoked by bootstrap, adapter layer, or app runtime. PG migrations at migration-pg/ are never applied at startup. This means any new PG deployment would have no schema.

summary: PG migration folder has only 1 migration vs 20 SQLite migrations
details: migration-pg/ contains only the initial schema snapshot (yellow_molly_hayes). The SQLite migration folder has 20 incremental migrations spanning months. No tooling exists to generate PG-compatible migrations from the shared .sql.ts schema files.

summary: sync/event.sql.ts has no .pg.sql.ts counterpart — Event domain excluded from PG schema
details: The sync event tables (EventSequenceTable, EventTable) are defined in event.sql.ts but there's no event.pg.sql.ts re-export. These tables appear in the PG initial migration so they were included at snapshot time, but there's no PG schema entry for any future evolution.

The DataMigrationTable schema has no PG re-export. The data-migration.ts module uses Database.use() (SQLite path) directly instead of the DatabaseAdapter.Service, meaning data migrations bypass the adapter layer entirely.

summary: No PG-specific drizzle config exists — PG migration generation requires manual work
details: drizzle.config.ts only configures 'sqlite' dialect. There's no drizzle.config.pg.ts or equivalent. The single PG migration in migration-pg/ was generated outside this config. To generate future PG migrations, a separate PG drizzle config would need to be created using drizzle-orm/pg-core schema definitions.

summary: PgAdapter does not apply migrations at creation time
details: makePgAdapter() in adapter.ts calls initPg() but never calls applyMigrations(). This means even if the PgAdapter layer is selected, no schema migrations are run. The SQLite path also does not apply migrations.
